summaryZh: 工资族大多不用自己报税——公司会从工资源泉徴収，年末调整搞定。需要自己申告的主要是：副业、医疗费扣除、首次买房、自由职业者。
summaryEn: Company employees usually don't file themselves — payroll withholding + year-end adjustment cover it. Filing is mainly for side income, medical deductions, home purchase, or freelance work.
pointsZh: 所得税：公司每月源泉徴収，年末调整后多退少补
住民税：按上一年收入算，今年 6 月起每月从工资扣
確定申告：每年 2 月 16 日 – 3 月 15 日，可用 e-Tax 在线
医疗费扣除：年医疗费超过 10 万円可申报扣除（要留收据）
ふるさと納税：用住民税抵扣捐给地方政府，还能拿当地特产
pointsEn: Income tax: withheld monthly by employer, reconciled at year-end
Resident tax: based on prior-year income, deducted monthly from salary starting June
Final filing: Feb 16 – Mar 15, can use e-Tax online
Medical deduction: annual medical costs over ¥100k can be deducted (keep receipts)
Furusato Nozei: donate to municipalities for tax credit + local specialty gifts
tipZh: マイナンバーカード 可以让你用 e-Tax 在家申报，不用去税务署排队。
tipEn: With a My Number Card, you can file via e-Tax from home — no queueing at the tax office.

summaryZh: 日本对税务居民按「过去 10 年内居住 5 年」划线：超过 5 年的就成「永住者（税法意义）」，必须申报包括海外的全球收入。这跟你有没有永住签证无关。
summaryEn: Japan classifies tax residents by the 5-out-of-10-years rule. If you've lived here more than 5 years in the past 10, you become a 'permanent resident' for tax purposes (separate from visa status) and must report worldwide income.
pointsZh: 判断标准是「物理居住时间」，跟你拿什么签证、有没有永住权无关
非永住者（≤5 年）：只对日本国内收入＋汇入日本的海外收入纳税
永住者（>5 年）：全球所有收入都要申报
国外已交的税可通过「外国税额控除」抵扣，不会双重征税
pointsEn: Status is decided by physical residency time — visa type doesn't matter
Non-permanent resident (≤5 yrs): taxed on Japan-source income + foreign income remitted to Japan
Permanent resident for tax (>5 yrs): all worldwide income reportable
Foreign taxes already paid can be offset via Foreign Tax Credit — no double taxation
tipZh: 快住满 5 年那年如果有大额海外资产或收入，应该提前找会计咨询——这是最容易被忽视的「跳一档」时刻，错过后整个税务结构变化很大。
tipEn: If you're approaching the 5-year mark with significant foreign assets or income, talk to an accountant ahead of time — it's the most commonly missed 'tax bracket jump'.

titleZh: 国外転出時課税・离日税
summaryZh: 在日本住满 5 年（同税法永住者标准）且持有 1 亿日元以上有价证券的人，离开日本时要按「视同出售」对未实现资本利得纳税。2015 年 7 月开始。
summaryEn: Tax residents who've lived in Japan 5+ years (same threshold as tax-status permanent resident) and hold ¥100M+ in eligible securities owe tax on unrealized capital gains when leaving — assets are treated as if sold. Started July 2015.
pointsZh: 对象：与税法永住者同样的 5/10 年标准 + 有价证券・匿名組合出資・衍生品合计 ≥ 1 亿円
应税：股票、投信、未决算的信用交易・デリバティブ等的未实现利得（按离日时市价 − 取得成本）
不在范围：不動産、銀行存款、加密货币等（截至 2025 现行规定）
可担保 + 设纳税管理人延期 5 年（可再延长至 10 年）；5 年内回日且仍持有该资产，可申请撤销
离日前需提交「出国時確定申告」，与普通报税不同
pointsEn: Who: same 5/10-year resident threshold + ¥100M in eligible securities/derivatives
What: unrealized gains on stocks, mutual funds, unsettled credit/derivative positions (market value at departure − cost basis)
Not covered: real estate, bank deposits, crypto, etc. (as of 2025)
5-year deferral available with collateral + tax representative (extendable to 10 yrs); reversible if you return within 5 yrs still holding the assets
A separate 'departure tax return' is required before leaving Japan
tipZh: 1 亿円门槛看起来高，但 IT 行业 RSU / 期权较多的话很容易跨过去。如果你接近这条线，建议至少提前半年咨询税理士。
tipEn: The ¥100M threshold sounds high but is easy to cross with tech-sector RSUs or stock options. If you're approaching it, consult a tax accountant at least 6 months before leaving.

summaryZh: 日本对每人每年收受的赠与按累进税率征税。基础免税额是 110 万円/年/受赠者，超过部分由「收钱的人」申告纳税。家人之间也算。
summaryEn: Japan taxes gifts received per person per year at progressive rates. The basic annual exemption is ¥1.1M per recipient — the recipient (not the giver) files and pays. Family gifts count.
pointsZh: 免税额：每位受赠者每年（1/1–12/31）收的合计 ≤ 110 万円 → 免税
纳税人：受赠者本人，次年 2/1–3/15 申告
扶養義務 范围内的家人日常生活费・学费不算赠与
几个大额通道：住宅资金（至 1000 万）、教育资金一括赠与（至 1500 万）、配偶居住房产（至 2000 万）
pointsEn: Exemption: total per recipient per year (Jan–Dec) ≤ ¥1.1M → tax-free
Filer: the recipient, Feb 1 – Mar 15 next year
Regular living and education expenses between family members aren't gifts (fuyō-gimu / duty of support)
Big-ticket exemptions: housing fund (up to ¥10M), lump-sum education (up to ¥15M), spouse residential property (¥20M)
tipZh: 2024 改革后，给予人死前 3–7 年内的赠与会被「生前贈与加算」拉回遗产税计算——临终前几年靠「每年 110 万 trickle 给孩子」避税的做法已经失效，需要长期规划。
tipEn: After the 2024 reform, gifts made within 3–7 years before the giver's death are pulled back into the inheritance tax base — the 'trickle ¥1.1M each year' strategy doesn't work in the final years. Long-term planning needed.
